magadheesan_velu.name: Magadheesan Velu
magadheesan_velu.summary:
Out of Tennessee, Magadheesan Velu has climbed to the top of the mountain at Junior Nationals, the crown jewel of the American junior badminton season in BD U11. That title represents the culmination of grueling competition against the very best in the country. With 7 career victories across 12 tournaments, Magadheesan has proven the ability to deliver when it matters most.

Notably, Magadheesan has entered higher age groups at 2 tournaments, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

What makes Magadheesan especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines — boasting 1 singles title, 4 doubles titles, 2 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals champion (BD U11) • 2 ORC titles.

The numbers tell a compelling story: Magadheesan boasts 48 straight-games victories, showing the ability to close out matches decisively, a solid 68% win rate over 77 matches, a biggest win margin of 33 points.

With 9 different doubles partners over the years, Magadheesan adapts to anyone:
  • Riana Soprala (current primary partner) — 9 events together this season alone with 1 title, building serious momentum (9 total events in XD, across 2 seasons)
  • Boqian Zheng (current primary partner) — 6 events together this season alone with 3 titles, building serious momentum (6 total events in BD, across 2 seasons)

Magadheesan has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Magadheesan has competed across 4 different regions (Midwest, NE, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

Magadheesan has been seeded #1 or #2 in 7 events — recognition by tournament committees that this player consistently ranks among the very best in the draw.

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Magadheesan's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

maggie_zang.name: Maggie Zang
maggie_zang.summary:
Hailing from California, Maggie Zang has reached the pinnacle of American junior badminton — selected for the U.S. Junior National Team. Winning at the Selection Event (U13 GS, U13 GD) is the ultimate validation: it means being chosen to represent the country on the international stage. With 9 career titles across 35 tournaments, this is a player who delivers when everything is on the line.

Maggie is known for playing up, regularly entering higher age groups and testing against older, more experienced opponents (5 tournaments with multi-age-group entries). That willingness to seek out tougher competition accelerates development in ways that playing it safe never can.

A true triple-threat, Maggie competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 1 singles title, 3 doubles titles, 4 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event champion (U13 GS, U13 GD) — selected to represent the USA • Junior Nationals top-2 (GS U15) • 4 ORC titles.

Maggie has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 28 different players across the career:
  • Jennifer Bangcaya (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in GD/U17 (10 events, best: #3, spanning 3 seasons)
  • Yifan Liu (current primary partner) — currently teaming up in XD (8 events, best: #2, spanning 4 seasons)
  • Gavin Le (current partner) — currently teaming up in XD (3 events, best: #2, across 2 seasons)
  • Jayden Mar (current partner) — 4 events, 1 title together in U13/XD, across 2 seasons

Maggie is peaking at the right time in 2025-2026, with 1 title across 7 tournaments — a clear sign that Maggie is still ascending.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Maggie has competed across U13, U15, U17 and U19, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Maggie has competed across 5 different regions (NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 12 seeded entries across the career, Maggie consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

Over 5 seasons and 9 titles, Maggie has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.
